title: Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary
tagline: Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary (established in 1979, also known as the Jawaharlal Nehru Bustard Sanctuary of Maharashtra) is a wildlife sanctuary for the great Indian bustard (Arde
overview.description: Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary (established in 1979, also known as the Jawaharlal Nehru Bustard Sanctuary of Maharashtra) is a wildlife sanctuary for the great Indian bustard (Ardeotis nigriceps) at Solapur, Maharashtra, India. The land is drought-prone and semi-arid. It is in the Deccan thorn scrub forests ecoregion. Maharashtra is one of the six states of India where great Indian bustards (Ardeotis nigriceps) are still seen.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (137 km) and the nearest railway station is Solapur Junction (107 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Great Indian Bustard Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Warkatne, Karmala, Arjunnagar.
